##         Timestamp Year Month Day Hour  PM2.5
## 1 11/7/2017 12:00 2017    11   7   12  64.51
## 2 11/7/2017 13:00 2017    11   7   13  69.95
## 3 11/7/2017 14:00 2017    11   7   14  92.79
## 4 11/7/2017 15:00 2017    11   7   15 109.66
## 5 11/7/2017 16:00 2017    11   7   16 116.50
## 6 11/7/2017 17:00 2017    11   7   17 124.21
colSums(is.na(Data))
## Timestamp      Year     Month       Day      Hour     PM2.5 
##         0         0         0         0         0         0
# Ensure the column PM2.5 exists in the data
if ("PM2.5" %in% colnames(Data)) {
  # Calculate Q1, Q3, IQR, and bounds
  Q1 <- quantile(Data$PM2.5, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
  Q3 <- quantile(Data$PM2.5, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
  IQR_value <- Q3 - Q1
  
  lower_bound <- Q1 - 1.5 * IQR_value
  upper_bound <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR_value
  
  # Compute the median value
  median_value <- median(Data$PM2.5, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Replace outliers with the median value
  Data_df <- Data %>%
    mutate(PM2.5 = ifelse(PM2.5 < lower_bound | PM2.5 > upper_bound, median_value, PM2.5))
# Optionally, view the cleaned data
  print(Data_df)
  
} else {
  stop("Dataset is empty or column 'PM2.5' not found in the dataset!")
}

## 16659  1/13/2020 11:00 2020     1  13   11  59.71
## 16660  1/13/2020 12:00 2020     1  13   12  61.84
## 16661  1/13/2020 13:00 2020     1  13   13  60.62
## 16662  1/13/2020 14:00 2020     1  13   14  63.35
## 16663  1/13/2020 15:00 2020     1  13   15  74.53
## 16664  1/13/2020 16:00 2020     1  13   16  76.17
## 16665  1/13/2020 17:00 2020     1  13   17  93.51
## 16666  1/13/2020 18:00 2020     1  13   18  96.19
##  [ reached 'max' / getOption("max.print") -- omitted 19526 rows ]
# Plot the filtered PM2.5 data
library(ggplot2)
# Convert the 'Timestamp' column to Date format
Data_df$Date <- as.Date(Data_df$Timestamp, format = "%m/%d/%Y %H:%M")
str(Data_df$Date)
##  Date[1:36192], format: "2017-11-07" "2017-11-07" "2017-11-07" "2017-11-07" "2017-11-07" ...
# Aggregate data to the monthly level
library(dplyr)

Data_df <- Data_df %>% 
  mutate(year_month = format(Date, "%Y-%m")) %>% 
  group_by(year_month) %>% 
  summarise(PM2.5 = mean(PM2.5, na.rm = TRUE))

# Display the first few rows
head(Data_df)
## # A tibble: 6 × 2
##   year_month PM2.5
##   <chr>      <dbl>
## 1 2017-11     79.5
## 2 2017-12     83.3
## 3 2018-01     83.7
## 4 2018-02     72.5
## 5 2018-03     64.8
## 6 2018-04     53.2
#log transformation
log <- log(Data_df$PM2.5)
# Load ggplot2 for visualization
library(ggplot2)

# Line plot to visualize PM2.5 over the months
ggplot(Data_df, aes(x = as.Date(paste0(year_month, "-01")), y = PM2.5)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Monthly Average PM2.5 Levels Over the Years", x = "Year-Month", y = "PM2.5 Level") +
  theme_minimal()

#the fluctuation shows yearly seasonality and overall decreasing trend from 2018 to 2020 and do not shows a clear pattern
# Convert to time series format
PM2.5_ts <- ts(Data_df$PM2.5, 
              start = c(as.numeric(substr(min(Data_df$year_month), 1, 4)), 
                        as.numeric(substr(min(Data_df$year_month), 6, 7))), 
              frequency = 12)

# Decompose time series
decomposed_ts <- decompose(PM2.5_ts, type = "multiplicative")

# Plot the decomposition
plot(decomposed_ts)

# Compute basic statistical properties
mean_pm25 <- mean(Data_df$PM2.5, na.rm = TRUE)
var_pm25 <- var(Data_df$PM2.5, na.rm = TRUE)

# Compute and plot autocorrelation
autocorr_pm25 <- acf(Data_df$PM2.5,lag.max = 60, plot = TRUE)

# Display statistics
print(paste("Mean:", mean_pm25))
## [1] "Mean: 47.8428181408359"
print(paste("Variance:", var_pm25))
## [1] "Variance: 411.121117394095"
# Conduct Augmented Dickey-Fuller (ADF) test for stationarity
adf_test <- adf.test(PM2.5_ts)
## Warning in adf.test(PM2.5_ts): p-value smaller than printed p-value
print(adf_test)
## 
##  Augmented Dickey-Fuller Test
## 
## data:  PM2.5_ts
## Dickey-Fuller = -4.869, Lag order = 3, p-value = 0.01
## alternative hypothesis: stationary
#as you can see the output it shows P-value = 0.01 which means it's stationary
# cheking the ACF and PACF plot to find the order (p,d,q), 
acf(PM2.5_ts, main = "Autocorrelation Function", lag.max = 48)

pacf(PM2.5_ts, main = "Partial Autocorrelation Function", lag.max = 48)

#after checking the ACF and PACF plot it show seasonality in the ACF plot, as you can see in PACF plot the first lag shows high positive partial autocorrelation.
# Number of months in your dataset
total_length <- length(PM2.5_ts)
# Split the dataset into training and testing data
train_size <- floor(0.8 * length(PM2.5_ts))
train_data <- PM2.5_ts[1:train_size]
test_data <- PM2.5_ts[(train_size + 1):length(PM2.5_ts)]

# Check the split
length(train_data)  # Should be 44 months (80%)
## [1] 44
length(test_data)   # Should be 12 months (20%)
## [1] 12
plot.ts(train_data, main = "Time Series Plot", col = "blue")

library(forecast)
#auto arima capture all the seasonality and automatically remove so there is no need to apply seasonal differncing.
# Convert data to time series 
train_ts <- ts(train_data, frequency = 12) 

# Automatically fit SARIMA model
sarima_model <- auto.arima(train_ts, seasonal = TRUE)
# Print model summary
summary(sarima_model)
## Series: train_ts 
## ARIMA(0,1,1)(1,1,0)[12] 
## 
## Coefficients:
##           ma1     sar1
##       -0.3377  -0.3851
## s.e.   0.1933   0.2025
## 
## sigma^2 = 26.63:  log likelihood = -94.85
## AIC=195.7   AICc=196.59   BIC=200
## 
## Training set error measures:
##                     ME     RMSE      MAE       MPE     MAPE      MASE
## Training set 0.2826582 4.189759 2.949201 0.9628462 8.029742 0.4012517
##                    ACF1
## Training set 0.02051815
# Check model diagnostics
checkresiduals(sarima_model)

## 
##  Ljung-Box test
## 
## data:  Residuals from ARIMA(0,1,1)(1,1,0)[12]
## Q* = 8.4818, df = 7, p-value = 0.292
## 
## Model df: 2.   Total lags used: 9
ets_model <- ets(train_data)
summary(ets_model)
## ETS(A,A,N) 
## 
## Call:
## ets(y = train_data)
## 
##   Smoothing parameters:
##     alpha = 0.9999 
##     beta  = 0.9999 
## 
##   Initial states:
##     l = 75.0953 
##     b = 5.3195 
## 
##   sigma:  9.67
## 
##      AIC     AICc      BIC 
## 371.9855 373.5644 380.9064 
## 
## Training set error measures:
##                     ME     RMSE     MAE      MPE     MAPE     MASE      ACF1
## Training set -0.199503 9.220015 7.24771 3.492546 17.28184 0.788639 0.1203609
checkresiduals(ets_model)

## 
##  Ljung-Box test
## 
## data:  Residuals from ETS(A,A,N)
## Q* = 15.69, df = 9, p-value = 0.07363
## 
## Model df: 0.   Total lags used: 9
library(forecast)

# Forecast from each model
sarima_forecast <- forecast(sarima_model, h = length(test_data))
ets_forecast <- forecast(ets_model, h = length(test_data))

# Calculate accuracy metrics
sarima_accuracy <- accuracy(sarima_forecast, test_data)
ets_accuracy <- accuracy(ets_forecast, test_data)

# Display results
sarima_accuracy
##                       ME     RMSE      MAE       MPE      MAPE      MASE
## Training set  0.28265823 4.189759 2.949201 0.9628462  8.029742 0.3209090
## Test set     -0.04704323 8.196055 6.902789 1.0077413 16.582014 0.7511074
##                    ACF1
## Training set 0.02051815
## Test set             NA
ets_accuracy
##                     ME      RMSE      MAE       MPE     MAPE     MASE      ACF1
## Training set -0.199503  9.220015  7.24771  3.492546 17.28184 0.788639 0.1203609
## Test set     40.738183 47.138808 40.95250 81.800686 82.81774 4.456131        NA
#as the results of accuracy shows the MAPE value in SARIMA model is lower than the MAPE value in ets model which suggest that Sarima model is the best fit for the data set.
library(forecast)

# Generate forecasts for the next 3 months
sarima_forecast <- forecast(sarima_model, h = 3)

# Plot the forecast
plot(sarima_forecast, main = "SARIMA Forecast vs Actual Data", xlab = "Time", ylab = "PM2.5", col = "blue")

# Add a legend to the plot
legend("topright", legend = c("Forecast", "Actual Data"), col = c("blue", "darkgreen"), lty = 1, cex = 0.8)

#checking the accuracy of the SARIMA model 
# Generate forecasts for the next 3 months using SARIMA model
sarima_forecast <- forecast(sarima_model,h = length(test_data))

# Calculate accuracy between forecasted and actual test data
accuracy_metrics <- accuracy(sarima_forecast, test_data)

# Print accuracy metrics
print(accuracy_metrics)
##                       ME     RMSE      MAE       MPE      MAPE      MASE
## Training set  0.28265823 4.189759 2.949201 0.9628462  8.029742 0.3209090
## Test set     -0.04704323 8.196055 6.902789 1.0077413 16.582014 0.7511074
##                    ACF1
## Training set 0.02051815
## Test set             NA
#as the accuracy shows the MAPE , RMSE, MAE shows lower value specially the MAPE shows lower values which means that SARIMA model is the best fit model for this dataset.
